subject: [AAF] Use common secret in sms preload
commit_message:
[AAF] Use common secret in sms preload

Use common secret template and init container to fill config files for
job that preloads secrets to SMS.

Init container is prepared for temporary workaround required by the
OOF team to encrypt passwords before storing them in sms.

The only thing that has to be done is to instead of just assigning for
example:
export AAI_PASS=${AAI_PASS_PLAIN};

do

export AAI_PASS=`awesomeEncryptCommand ${AAI_PASS_PLAIN}`

Issue-ID: OPTFRA-720
Change-Id: I0ada7de0aa8710580ccf51cb9ab0822b81b4f46a
Signed-off-by: Krzysztof Opasiak <k.opasiak@samsung.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 4 on the queue.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/4564/

Patch Set 1:

The encryption command is inside the has code.  Do you have an example that we can use generate encrypted code?
Or! can we just use the encrypted in values.yaml, and then revert once the new release is created?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> The encryption command is inside the has code.  Do you have an example that we can use generate encrypted code?
> Or! can we just use the encrypted in values.yaml, and then revert once the new release is created?

ehhh and that's exactly why encrypting those password is so wrong and why it should never happen.

Aren't you using any kind of standard algorithm? AES or sth? If so then you can use for example openssl or anything else to do the encryption?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > The encryption command is inside the has code.  Do you have an example that we can use generate encrypted code?
> > Or! can we just use the encrypted in values.yaml, and then revert once the new release is created?
> 
> ehhh and that's exactly why encrypting those password is so wrong and why it should never happen.
> 
> Aren't you using any kind of standard algorithm? AES or sth? If so then you can use for example openssl or anything else to do the encryption?

The encryption is not a simple encrypt command, there is some extra stuff added to it..  
For now I am going to add the encrypted key, till the new release build is created.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1:
> > > 
> > > The encryption command is inside the has code.  Do you have an example that we can use generate encrypted code?
> > > Or! can we just use the encrypted in values.yaml, and then revert once the new release is created?
> > 
> > ehhh and that's exactly why encrypting those password is so wrong and why it should never happen.

> 
> The encryption is not a simple encrypt command, there is some extra stuff added to it..  
> For now I am going to add the encrypted key, till the new release build is created.

Now I'm starting to be seriously afraid what you mean by "encryption". Could you please point me to the place in the code where this encryption and decryption takes place?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1:
> > > 
> > > > Patch Set 1:
> > > > 
> > > > The encryption command is inside the has code.  Do you have an example that we can use generate encrypted code?
> > > > Or! can we just use the encrypted in values.yaml, and then revert once the new release is created?
> > > 
> > > ehhh and that's exactly why encrypting those password is so wrong and why it should never happen.
> > > 
> > > Aren't you using any kind of standard algorithm? AES or sth? If so then you can use for example openssl or anything else to do the encryption?
> > 
> > The encryption is not a simple encrypt command, there is some extra stuff added to it..  
> > For now I am going to add the encrypted key, till the new release build is created.
> 
> Now I'm starting to be seriously afraid what you mean by "encryption". Could you please point me to the place in the code where this encryption and decryption takes place?

I wish we were using some esoteric encryption 😊 But no, we are simply using the AES encryption provided pycrypto-2.6.1/pycryptodome-3.9.6 (https://pypi.org/project/pycrypto/)

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1:
> > > 
> > > > Patch Set 1:
> > > > 
> > > > The encryption command is inside the has code.  Do you have an example that we can use generate encrypted code?
> > > > Or! can we just use the encrypted in values.yaml, and then revert once the new release is created?
> > > 
> > > ehhh and that's exactly why encrypting those password is so wrong and why it should never happen.
> > > 
> > > Aren't you using any kind of standard algorithm? AES or sth? If so then you can use for example openssl or anything else to do the encryption?
> > 
> > The encryption is not a simple encrypt command, there is some extra stuff added to it..  
> > For now I am going to add the encrypted key, till the new release build is created.
> 
> Now I'm starting to be seriously afraid what you mean by "encryption". Could you please point me to the place in the code where this encryption and decryption takes place?

For example this is the code thats used to encrypt/decrypt the password (given a key).  When we generate the encrypted string, its not just a AES string that is getting created, its a combination of certain things.

https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=optf/has.git;a=blob;f=conductor/conductor/common/utils/cipherUtils.py;h=21a33b4f307380be88c2bf8e03f0ce52b67f2913;hb=refs/heads/master

We can ignore this issue. I have created the new release versions that will use unencrypted passwords.
